statement.markdown: You are given an array of integers A of size N where A[i] represents the i-th integer in the array. You are also given an integer k, which represents the number of subarrays in the array that you can get the element sum from. You need to calculate the minimum possible sum of k subarrays from the array A.
statement.inputFormat:
The input will consist of the number of elements n in the first line, followed by the n space separated integers A[1], A[2], ... A[n]. k will be given in the next line.
Example:
5
1 2 3 4 5
2
statement.outputFormat:
You need to print the minimum sum of the k subarrays from A.
Example:
10
statement.notes: * The sum of an empty subarray is 0.

hints.text: The bug is in the logic of the function. It should find the minimum sum of k subarrays.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: For each subarray, the minimum sum will be the sum of its elements.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to try all possible subarrays and calculate their sums
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a prefix sum array to calculate the sum of each subarray.
commonMistakes: Incorrect logic for calculating the minimum sum

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, A: list[int], k: int) -> int:
